title: gilia splendens subsp. grantii
intro: Gilia splendens subsp. grantii, also known as Grant's gilia or Grant's bee plant, is a flowering plant in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Gilia splendens subsp. grantii has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, and it has been used to treat conditions like bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
